title: Our Predictions for the Top 7 Blue-Chip Stocks for 2023
text: America's economic statistics continue to suggest that fears about an imminent recession and a corporate earnings collapse are way overblown. For example, during the recent holiday shopping season, sales climbed 7.6% year-over-year.

title: Booking Holdings' Stock Down 7% Over Ten Days. What's Next?
text: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), the world's largest online travel agency that offers services from lodging to airline tickets to car rentals, has declined by 7% over the last ten trading days (two weeks) and currently stands at around $1939.

title: Booking Holdings: What Are Your Plans For The Holidays?
text: We're buy-rated on Booking Holdings Inc., as we expect the company to rebound in 2023. We believe Booking is now better positioned to outperform, as pent-up demand for travel skyrockets post-pandemic, and we believe the company's Q3 2022 earnings report showed solid demand tailwinds.

title: Is the travel boom fading? Analysts are getting more skeptical about 2023.
text: Even as airline executives crank up their enthusiasm about the travel rebound to maximum volume, analysts see cracks elsewhere in the industry.

title: Why Booking Holdings, Southwest Airlines, and Uber Fell Today
text: Anxiety about the end of the travel boom is hurting these three mobility stocks today.

title: Booking Holdings (BKNG) to Lure Travelers With Recent Tie-Ups
text: Booking Holdings' (BKNG) brand Priceline collaborates with British Airways and Iberia to provide travelers with the best domestic and international travel deals.
